---
title: ""
page-layout: full

listing:
  - id: home-news
    contents: news/*/index.qmd
    sort: "date desc"
    template: assets/listings/news-listing-scroll.ejs.md
    fields: [title, date, description, categories, path]
    max-items: 6

  - id: home-publications
    contents: publications/*/*/index.qmd
    sort:
      - "pub-status asc"
      - "date desc"
    template: assets/listings/pub-listing-scroll.ejs.md
    fields: [title, pub-year, pub-authors, venue, pub-status, pub-type, pdf, path]
    max-items: 6
---

::::::: hero-header
::: hero-logo
![](images/lablogo.png){alt="CALM Lab logo"}
:::

::::: hero-text
::: hero-title
CALM LAB
:::

::: hero-subtitle
Control of Attention in Life and Mind
:::
:::::
:::::::

::: hero-intro
The CALM Lab studies attention control across contexts of life and mind, combining cognitive science, neuroscience, and contemplative approaches to understand how attention shapes thought, success, and well-being.
:::

:::::::::::: {.grid-3 .section-shell}
::::: {.card .card-interactive .quick-card .is-blue}
<a href="team/index.qmd">

::: {.icon-badge .icon-badge-sm}
<i class="bi bi-people"></i>
:::

### Team

Meet the researchers and students working in the CALM Lab.

[View Team →]{.link-cta}</a>
:::::

::::: {.card .card-interactive .quick-card .is-yellow}
<a href="projects/index.qmd">

::: {.icon-badge .icon-badge-sm}
<i class="bi bi-diagram-3"></i>
:::

### Projects

Explore our research on attention control, brain networks, and mindfulness.

[View Projects →]{.link-cta}</a>

:::::

::::: {.card .card-interactive .quick-card .is-green}
<a href="contact.qmd">

::: {.icon-badge .icon-badge-sm}
<i class="bi bi-envelope"></i>
:::

### Connect

Interested in collaborating, joining the lab, or learning more about our work?

[Get in Touch →]{.link-cta}</a>

:::::

::::::::::::

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# Latest News  {style="text-align: center; color: var(--color-primary);"}

::: {#home-news}
:::

::: listing-more

<a href="/news/" class="link-cta">View All News →</a>

:::

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# Research {style="text-align: center; color: var(--color-primary);"}

::: {style="text-align: center; color: var(--color-secondary); font-size: 1.25rem; font-weight: 700;"}
The CALM Lab’s research is organized around three interconnected areas that together aim to understand attention across contexts of life and mind.
:::

:::::::::::: {.grid-3 .section-shell}
::::: {.card .card-interactive .quick-card .is-blue}
<a href="/projects#cognitive-section">

### Cognitive Mechanisms

How do differences in the ability to control attention relate to cognition and well-being?

[View Project →]{.link-cta}</a>
:::::

::::: {.card .card-interactive .quick-card .is-yellow}
<a href="/projects#brain-section">

### Neural Mechanisms

What are the neural mechanisms underlying the ability to control attention?

[View Project →]{.link-cta}</a>

:::::

::::: {.card .card-interactive .quick-card .is-green}
<a href="/projects#mindfulness-section">

### Mindfulness

How can mindfulness and contemplative practices strengthen attention control and well-being?

[View Project →]{.link-cta}</a>

:::::

::::::::::::

### Latest Publications {style="text-align: center; color: var(--color-primary);"}

::: {#home-publications}
:::

::: listing-more
<a href="/publications/" class="link-cta">View All Publications →</a>
:::
